title: Ternary Operator
description: Write if-else statements in a single line.

slides.title: Short Hand If...Else 🤏
slides.content:
The ternary operator is a shorthand way to write a simple `if...else` statement.

It is known as the ternary operator because it consists of three operands.

slides.title: Syntax 📝
slides.content:
The syntax looks like this:

`variable = (condition) ? expressionTrue : expressionFalse;`

slides.title: Example
slides.content: Let's see the ternary operator in action. It checks if time is less than 18.

quizData.question: What is the output of the code snippet above?
quizData.explanation: Because time (20) < 18 is false, the expression returns the value on the right side of the colon (:), which is 'Good evening.'.

slides.title: Why Use It? 🤔
slides.content:
The ternary operator is excellent for assigning values to a variable based on a single condition, saving you multiple lines of code!

It replaces 5 lines of `if...else` code with just 1 line.

quizData.question: What symbol separates the 'true' and 'false' expressions in a ternary operator?
quizData.explanation: The question mark '?' follows the condition, and the colon ':' separates the true expression from the false expression.
